description: An examination of the use of laboratory or field experiments to examine questions of interest to economists.  How to design economic experiments and understand their results.  Understanding answers to key questions about economic policy, development economics, learning in uncertain situations, behavior in markets, market design, auctions, finance, altruism and selfishness, bargaining, and many other topics, as well as the processes that produced these answers, using principles from statistical models, survey design, game theory, understanding of incentives, and behavioral economics.
